chapter_title.en: Description of the Prayer From Its Beginning to Its End

text: 920 - وَ كَانَ رَسُولُ اَللَّهِ صَلَّى اَللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَ آلِهِ أَتَمَّ اَلنَّاسِ صَلاَةً وَ أَوْجَزَهُمْ كَانَ إِذَا دَخَلَ فِي صَلاَةٍ قَالَ «اَللَّهُ أَكْبَرُ «بِسْمِ اَللّٰهِ اَلرَّحْمٰنِ اَلرَّحِيمِ».

en.bab-ul-qaim-publications:
Hadith.920 - "The Messenger of Allah (sw) was the most complete in performing prayer and the most concise.
When he began his prayer, he would say: 'Allāhu Akbar (God is the Greatest)' and then recite, 'Bismillāh al-Raḥmān al-Raḥīm (In the name of Allah (swt), the Most Gracious, the Most Merciful).'"
